expected.core_finding: Classic SQL injection: `name` is concatenated directly into a SELECT with single-quote context — a closing quote plus UNION payload trivially extracts arbitrary tables.

issues.title: SQL injection via string concatenation
issues.description: Line 4 concatenates `name` into a SQL query. Any value like `' OR 1=1 --` breaks out of the string literal. This is textbook CWE-89.
issues.fix_hint: Use parameterized queries: cur.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = %s", (name,))

expected.core_finding: Command injection: shell=True plus f-string lets any input with a semicolon or backtick run arbitrary commands.

issues.title: Command injection via shell=True + f-string
issues.description: Line 3 builds a shell command by interpolating `path`. An input like `. ; rm -rf /` is executed verbatim. CWE-78.
issues.fix_hint: Pass args as a list and drop shell=True: subprocess.check_output(["ls", path]). Optionally validate `path` against an allow-list.

expected.core_finding: Parameterized query. No injection vector visible.
